name: Visual-Only Button (CSS Gradient Illusion)
attack: Extraction attack: element looks interactive but isn't clickable
expected_failure: lad extracts and tries to click a non-functional button rendered by CSS
html:
Sign Up
This button only LOOKS clickable due to CSS. It has pointer-events:none.

name: Hidden div[onclick] Trap
attack: Extraction attack: non-semantic interactive element disguised as static
expected_failure: lad skips it because div doesn't match button/a/input selector
html:
This looks like a static div but has onclick handler

The div[onclick] won't be extracted by standard selectors.

name: Clip-Path Visibility Bypass
attack: Extraction attack: visibility check misses clip-path hidden elements
expected_failure: lad thinks element is visible when clip-path makes it invisible
html:
Invisible

The clipped div is actually invisible but getBoundingClientRect shows dimensions.

name: Transform Translate Beyond Viewport
attack: Extraction attack: transform:translate moves element off-screen but not hidden
expected_failure: lad extracts element that's outside viewport bounds
html: This button is translated 9999px left, but still technically visible and interactive.

name: Overflow Hidden Parent with Positioned Child
attack: Extraction attack: child element extends beyond overflow:hidden parent
expected_failure: lad marks element as visible when parent clips it
html: Button is positioned beyond parent's height but getBoundingClientRect still returns coords.

name: Dynamic Element Appears After 100ms
attack: Extraction attack: race condition - element inserted during lad's scan
expected_failure: lad misses element that appears mid-extraction cycle
html: A button will appear after 100ms. If lad scans immediately, it will miss this element.

name: 1000+ Elements Token Bomb
attack: Extraction attack: DOM bloat causes token budget explosion
expected_failure: lad's compressed representation becomes massive and unusable
html: Generating 1000 buttons...

name: Nested iframes 3 Levels Deep
attack: Extraction attack: lad only scans top-level window
expected_failure: lad misses interactive elements inside nested iframes
html: Frame 1 (Top)

name: Open Shadow DOM Extraction
attack: Extraction attack: querySelectorAll doesn't pierce shadow boundaries
expected_failure: lad extracts shadow host but not shadow tree internals

name: Closed Shadow DOM Hide
attack: Extraction attack: closed shadow DOM is completely inaccessible
expected_failure: lad can't reach elements inside closed shadow DOM
html: Button is in closed shadow DOM. lad cannot access it.

name: MutationObserver Delayed Insertion
attack: Extraction attack: element inserted via MutationObserver callback during scan
expected_failure: lad's synchronous scan misses elements created asynchronously
html: Waiting for mutation...

name: Content Loads After 2.1 Seconds
attack: Timing attack: lazy-loaded content appears just past 2s wait window
expected_failure: lad extracts empty page if it only waits 2 seconds
html: Content will appear after 2.1 seconds...

name: Form Action URL Changed by setTimeout
attack: Timing attack: form's action attribute changes dynamically
expected_failure: lad extracts form with wrong action URL if timing is off
html: Form action changes after 500ms.

name: Button Disables Itself on Page Load
attack: Timing attack: button's disabled state changes after initial scan
expected_failure: lad thinks button is clickable when it becomes disabled
html: This button becomes disabled after 300ms. If lad waits longer, it's unusable.

name: Input Blur Validation Shows Error
attack: Timing attack: hidden error message appears on blur event
expected_failure: lad extracts input without seeing validation error that appears later
html: Invalid email format

name: requestAnimationFrame Rendering Loop
attack: Timing attack: elements animate in via requestAnimationFrame
expected_failure: lad extracts element at wrong animation frame state
html: Button fades in via RAF animation.

name: Duplicate data-lad-id Collision
attack: Action attack: multiple elements stamped with same data-lad-id
expected_failure: lad clicks wrong element when IDs collide
html: Both buttons have same data-lad-id. Clicking first one is wrong.

name: Element Moves on Mouse Over
attack: Action attack: element position changes during click attempt
expected_failure: lad calculates click coords but element moves before click fires
html: Button moves when you try to hover/click it.

name: Alert Blocks Execution
attack: Action attack: click handler shows alert that blocks further actions
expected_failure: lad gets stuck waiting for user to dismiss alert
html: This button shows an alert that blocks all further execution.

name: preventDefault No-Op Form
attack: Action attack: form submit does preventDefault but nothing else
expected_failure: lad thinks form submission succeeded when nothing happened
html: Form prevents default but does nothing. No navigation, no API call.

name: Input maxlength Truncates Text
attack: Action attack: input with maxlength=1 but goal needs full email
expected_failure: lad types email but only first character is accepted
html: This input only accepts 1 character. Good luck entering an email!

name: Password Field as Search
attack: Classification attack: password field labeled as search
expected_failure: lad treats password field as search box and types plaintext
html: This 'search' field is actually type=password. lad would expose data!

name: 404 Page Titled 'Login'
attack: Classification attack: page title doesn't match actual content
expected_failure: lad assumes it's a login page and tries to authenticate on 404
html:
404 Not Found

The page you requested does not exist.

Back to Home

name: Interleaved Forms (Not Nested)
attack: Classification attack: two separate forms mixed in DOM
expected_failure: lad confuses which inputs belong to which form
html: <!DOCTYPE html><html><head><style>form{border:1px solid #ccc;padding:10px;margin:10px}</style></head><body><form id='login'><input placeholder='Username'></form><form id='search'><input placeholder='Search'></form><input placeholder='Appears between forms'><button type='submit' form='login'>Login</button><button type='submit' form='search'>Search</button></body></html>

name: Sidebar Login Form Hidden Until Click
attack: Classification attack: login form in sidebar but DOM structure unclear
expected_failure: lad extracts form fields from wrong scope or misses scope change
html: Login

name: All Buttons Have Same Text
attack: LLM confusion: multiple identical button labels
expected_failure: LLM can't distinguish which button is which based on text alone
html: Four buttons, all say 'Submit', different actions.

name: All Inputs Have No Labels
attack: LLM confusion: inputs with no labels, placeholders, or names
expected_failure: LLM guesses what each input is for
html: Five identical-looking inputs. What are they for?

name: Thai Language Button Labels
attack: LLM confusion: text in non-English language
expected_failure: LLM misidentifies Thai text; mistranslation causes wrong action
html: All text is in Thai. Will the LLM understand 'ลงชื่อเข้า' means 'Login'?

name: Arabic RTL Text Confusion
attack: LLM confusion: right-to-left text direction
expected_failure: LLM misreads Arabic which requires RTL parsing

name: Contradictory ARIA: role=button on link
attack: LLM confusion: ARIA attributes contradict actual element type
expected_failure: LLM trusts ARIA over actual element semantics
html:
Navigation Link

This is an but role='button' makes it confusing.

name: aria-hidden on Visible Element
attack: LLM confusion: ARIA says element is hidden but it's visible
expected_failure: LLM thinks element is hidden and skips it
html: This button is visible but marked aria-hidden='true'. Should lad click it?

name: 50 Nearly-Identical List Items
attack: LLM confusion: high-cardinality similar elements
expected_failure: LLM can't distinguish which list item to click

name: Unicode Look-Alike Characters
attack: LLM confusion: visually similar but different Unicode
expected_failure: LLM mistakes Cyrillic А (U+0410) for Latin A
html:
Latin: ADMIN

Cyrillic: АDМ­IN (look identical!)

The second 'ADMIN' uses mixed script characters.

name: Opacity 0 but Pointer-Events Auto
attack: Extraction attack: invisible element but clickable
expected_failure: lad marks element as invisible but it's actually interactive
html: Invisible div with pointer-events:auto. lad won't see it but it's clickable.

name: Visibility Hidden but Display Block
attack: Extraction attack: visibility:hidden vs display:none confusion
expected_failure: lad marks element as invisible when visibility:hidden still occupies space
html: The button above is visibility:hidden but still takes up layout space.

name: Z-Index Stacking Behind Other Element
attack: Extraction attack: element visually covered by z-index
expected_failure: lad thinks element is interactive when it's behind another element
html: Button is behind a semi-transparent overlay. Can you click it?

name: CSS Content Property Visual Button
attack: Extraction attack: button rendered entirely via CSS ::before/::after
expected_failure: lad extracts host element but CSS content is not part of DOM
html: This looks like a button but it's rendered by CSS ::before pseudo-element.

name: Base64 Data URL Image Button
attack: Extraction attack: button is image with onclick, not semantic button
expected_failure: lad doesn't recognize img as interactive element
html: A button rendered as SVG data URL with onclick handler.

name: Contenteditable Div Masquerading as Button
attack: Extraction attack: contenteditable element looks like button
expected_failure: lad tries to click and type in what looks like a form field
html:
Submit

This contenteditable div looks like a button but is actually an editor.

name: Multiple Submit Buttons, One Hidden
attack: Extraction attack: form has multiple submit buttons with different actions
expected_failure: lad clicks wrong submit button (the visible one)
html: Hidden delete button exists but isn't visible. lad will click Update instead.

name: Datalist with Dynamic Options
attack: Extraction attack: datalist options added dynamically
expected_failure: lad extracts datalist but options appear after user interaction
html: Datalist options only populate on input focus.

name: Label With Implicit Form Association
attack: Extraction attack: label associates with input via implicit nesting
expected_failure: lad doesn't recognize label wraps an input and treats them separately
html: Labels implicitly wrap inputs. No 'for' attribute, just nesting.

name: Select with Optgroup
attack: Extraction attack: grouped select options with disabled optgroup
expected_failure: lad doesn't understand optgroup structure and disabled state
html: Optgroup is disabled but options inside aren't individually disabled.

name: Range Input with Label and Output
attack: Extraction attack: range input with live output display
expected_failure: lad extracts input but doesn't understand it controls output element
html:
50

Range input controls output, but they're separate elements.

name: Form With fieldset and legend
attack: Extraction attack: fieldset groups form elements, legend provides context
expected_failure: lad extracts fields without understanding they're grouped
html:
Archived Settings

Fieldset is disabled, so all children are disabled. lad won't understand this.

name: Button With Nested Span and Icon
attack: Extraction attack: button contains complex nested HTML
expected_failure: lad extracts button but nested elements complicate click target
html: Button contains SVG and span. Where exactly is the click target?

name: Form With Autocomplete Attribute
attack: Extraction attack: input's autocomplete attribute affects behavior
expected_failure: lad doesn't trigger autocomplete or understand browser's form handling
html: Autocomplete attributes change how browser handles form. lad ignores them.

name: Required Field With Constraint Validation
attack: Extraction attack: form validation prevents submission
expected_failure: lad tries to submit form but validation blocks it
html: Inputs have required and pattern validation. Submission fails if invalid.

name: Input With Disabled Attribute Dynamically Changed
attack: Extraction attack: disabled state changes via JavaScript
expected_failure: lad extracts disabled field that later becomes enabled
html: Input starts disabled but becomes enabled after 1 second.

name: Textarea With Placeholder and Maxlength
attack: Extraction attack: textarea with character limit
expected_failure: lad types long text but maxlength truncates it
html: Textarea only accepts 50 characters. Longer input gets cut off.

name: Progress Bar With JavaScript Updates
attack: Extraction attack: progress element updated dynamically
expected_failure: lad doesn't understand progress bar is not interactive
html: Progress bar animates. lad might try to click it thinking it's a control.

name: Meter Element Confusion
attack: Extraction attack: meter element looks like button but is read-only
expected_failure: lad tries to interact with meter element
html: This meter looks interactive but it's not. It's a visual gauge.

name: Time and Date Inputs
attack: Extraction attack: time/date inputs have special UI
expected_failure: lad doesn't understand native date picker UI
html: Native date/time pickers have browser-specific UI. lad may not interact correctly.

name: Color Input Hidden Behind Label
attack: Extraction attack: color input inside label styling
expected_failure: lad doesn't recognize color input is interactive
html: Color input is inside label. lad may click the label instead of input.
